summary: @fhkry/x-baileys hijacks installer's WhatsApp session to silently follow attacker-controlled newsletters and join an attacker group
details: This package is a fork of the Baileys WhatsApp library that adds covert actions abusing the installer's authenticated WhatsApp session. In lib/Socket/newsletter.js, on every socket creation a 2-minute timer fetches a base64-obfuscated URL that decodes to https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Fhkryy/Fhkry/refs/heads/main/peler.json, parses its entries as base64-encoded WhatsApp newsletter JIDs, and issues newsletterWMexQuery FOLLOW for each entry — silently making the connected WhatsApp account follow channels chosen by the attacker via a mutable remote list. Additionally, a connection.update listener installed inside makeNewsletterSocket calls sock.groupAcceptInvite on a hardcoded invite (https://chat.whatsapp.com/C8n8s4Yj1G42wdkAwDP7rP) whenever the connection opens, auto-joining the installer's account into an attacker-controlled group. The base64 obfuscation of the URL and the GitHub path under the author's own username (Fhkryy) confirm intent to evade scanners. Any caller who connects a real WhatsApp number through this library has their session weaponized to inflate the attacker's channels and group membership.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
